hypothesis: If a semi-supervised training objective combines supervised loss with an ensemble-consistency loss (each model minimizing distance to the detached ensemble mean with coupling weight γ>0), then it will yield significantly lower test error and improved calibration than supervised-only baselines and alternative SSL methods on QM9 and GNN+ molecular benchmarks under 10% label regimes, with performance gains that are robust across GCN/GIN/GatedGCN architectures and that increase with ensemble size up to M=32.
